page.title: Azimut 58 vs Sunseeker Predator 60: Marbella Pick

data.meta_description: Azimut 58 vs Sunseeker Predator Marbella: compare size, speed, capacity & price for your Marbella boat charter. Book the Azimut 58 Flybridge today.
data.summary: Compare the Azimut 58 Flybridge and Sunseeker Predator 60 for a Marbella boat charter. The Azimut 58 offers a flybridge, 12 pax capacity, and inclusive pricing, while the Predator 60 is a sportier option with similar capacity.
key_facts: Azimut 58 Flybridge: 17.6m, 12 pax, departs Puerto Banús
Azimut 58 vs Sunseeker Predator Marbella: both accommodate up to 12 guests
Azimut 58 Flybridge includes flybridge lounge, spacious sundeck
Sunseeker Predator 60 known for sporty performance and open layout
Azimut 58 charter price: on request via WhatsApp (all inclusive)
Sunseeker Predator 60 pricing on request via WhatsApp
Both boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, VAT

faq.q: What is the main difference between the Azimut 58 and Sunseeker Predator 60?
faq.a: The Azimut 58 Flybridge features a raised flybridge deck with seating and sun pads, ideal for socialising and panoramic views. The Sunseeker Predator 60 has a sportier, open-plan layout with a lower cockpit and a more aggressive design, prioritising speed and a sleek profile.

faq.q: Which boat is faster: Azimut 58 or Sunseeker Predator 60?
faq.a: The Sunseeker Predator 60 is generally faster due to its sportier hull and more powerful engine options, often reaching speeds over 35 knots. The Azimut 58 Flybridge is more cruising-oriented, with a top speed around 28-30 knots, offering a smoother ride at lower speeds.

faq.q: How many guests can each boat accommodate?
faq.a: Both the Azimut 58 Flybridge and the Sunseeker Predator 60 can accommodate up to 12 guests for day charters. The Azimut 58 has a maximum of 12 pax, while the Predator 60 also typically carries 12 guests, making them equally suitable for groups of that size.

faq.q: What is the price for chartering the Azimut 58 in Marbella?
faq.a: The Azimut 58 Flybridge is priced on request via WhatsApp — quotes within minutes for your date and group size. All prices include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% Spanish VAT.

faq.q: Does the Sunseeker Predator 60 have a flybridge?
faq.a: No, the Sunseeker Predator 60 does not have a flybridge. It features a large open cockpit with a sun pad aft and a helm deck with a retractable hardtop, offering a more integrated indoor-outdoor feel. The Azimut 58, by contrast, has a dedicated flybridge level.

faq.q: Where do these boats depart from in Marbella?
faq.a: Both the Azimut 58 Flybridge and the Sunseeker Predator 60 depart from Puerto Banús, the famous marina in Marbella. This puts you right at the heart of the Golden Mile, with easy access to the coast towards Estepona, Cabopino, and Sotogrande.

faq.q: Which boat is better for a luxury day charter?
faq.a: For a luxury day charter, the Azimut 58 Flybridge offers more deck space, a flybridge for sunbathing and dining, and a more relaxed cruising experience. The Sunseeker Predator 60 is better for those who prioritise sporty looks and a more intimate, cockpit-focused layout.

faq.q: Can I book the Sunseeker Predator 60 through Boat Rental Marbella?
faq.a: The Sunseeker Predator 60 is not currently listed in our fleet. We offer the Azimut 58 Flybridge as a premium option. For other similar-sized yachts, please contact us via WhatsApp to discuss availability and pricing.

data.body_html:
Choosing between the Azimut 58 Flybridge and the Sunseeker Predator 60 for a Marbella boat charter? Both are stunning 55-60ft yachts that turn heads in Puerto Banús, but they offer very different experiences. The Azimut 58 is a flybridge cruiser with a raised helm and upper deck, while the Predator 60 is a sportier, open-cockpit model. Below we compare size, capacity, speed, price, and layout to help you pick the right boat for your day on the Costa del Sol.

Size and capacity

The Azimut 58 Flybridge measures 17.6 metres (58 feet) and can carry up to 12 guests. The Sunseeker Predator 60 is slightly longer at 18.3 metres (60 feet) and also accommodates 12 passengers. Both are large enough for a comfortable group charter, but the Azimut 58’s flybridge adds an extra social deck that the Predator 60 lacks. If you want to spread out across three levels (bow, cockpit, flybridge), the Azimut 58 is the better choice.

Speed and performance

The Sunseeker Predator 60 is built for speed. With twin MAN or Volvo Penta engines, it can reach 35-38 knots, making it ideal for quick runs from Puerto Banús to Cala del Faro or Estepona. The Azimut 58 Flybridge is more of a cruising yacht, topping out around 28-30 knots. Both are powered by reliable diesel inboards, but the Azimut 58 offers a quieter, more stable ride at cruising speeds of 22-24 knots. For a relaxed day with lunch stops, the Azimut 58 is smoother; for a thrill ride, the Predator 60 wins.

Layout and amenities

The Azimut 58 Flybridge features a spacious flybridge with a second helm station, sun pads, and a dining table. Below deck, it has a large saloon with a galley, plus three cabins (master VIP, guest double, twin). The Sunseeker Predator 60 has a more open-plan cockpit with a sun pad aft and a helm deck with a retractable hardtop. Its interior is sportier, with a smaller saloon but a larger cockpit area. For sunbathing and entertaining, the Azimut 58’s flybridge is a clear advantage. The Predator 60 feels more intimate and driver-focused.

Charter pricing

The Azimut 58 Flybridge is available for charter with all-inclusive pricing quoted on request via WhatsApp. All prices include a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, light snacks, insurance, and 21% Spanish VAT. The Sunseeker Predator 60 is not in our fleet, but comparable sport yachts of that size typically cost €1,500-€2,500 for a half-day (4 hours) when booked through private owners. For a guaranteed, all-inclusive experience, the Azimut 58 offers better value.

Which boat is right for you?

Choose the Azimut 58 Flybridge if you want a luxurious, multi-deck cruising experience with ample sunbathing space and a flybridge for panoramic views. It’s perfect for groups who plan to anchor, swim, and enjoy lunch on board. Choose the Sunseeker Predator 60 if you prioritise sporty aesthetics, higher speed, and a more helm-focused layout. However, since we don’t charter the Predator 60, the Azimut 58 is the practical, bookable option that delivers a premium day out from Puerto Banús. Browse our full fleet of Marbella charter boats to compare other options.

Where you’ll cruise

Both boats depart from Puerto Banús, giving you direct access to the best spots on the Costa del Sol. Typical routes include cruising past the Golden Mile towards Cabopino, stopping at Cala Cortés or Cala del Faro for swimming, or heading south to Estepona and Sotogrande. The Azimut 58’s flybridge is especially enjoyable for spotting La Concha mountain on the horizon while you sip a chilled cava. For a full-day itinerary, many guests combine a swim stop with lunch at a chiringuito like Elviria or Playa de la Fontanilla.

Book the Azimut 58 today

Ready to experience the Azimut 58 Flybridge in Marbella? Check availability and book the Azimut 58 for your next charter. All bookings include skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks — no hidden extras. For larger groups, consider our Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) or Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). Contact us via WhatsApp for custom itineraries or to inquire about other yachts.

Frequently asked questions

What is the main difference between the Azimut 58 and Sunseeker Predator 60?

The Azimut 58 Flybridge features a raised flybridge deck with seating and sun pads, ideal for socialising and panoramic views. The Sunseeker Predator 60 has a sportier, open-plan layout with a lower cockpit and a more aggressive design, prioritising speed and a sleek profile.

Which boat is faster: Azimut 58 or Sunseeker Predator 60?

The Sunseeker Predator 60 is generally faster due to its sportier hull and more powerful engine options, often reaching speeds over 35 knots. The Azimut 58 Flybridge is more cruising-oriented, with a top speed around 28-30 knots, offering a smoother ride at lower speeds.

How many guests can each boat accommodate?

Both the Azimut 58 Flybridge and the Sunseeker Predator 60 can accommodate up to 12 guests for day charters. The Azimut 58 has a maximum of 12 pax, while the Predator 60 also typically carries 12 guests, making them equally suitable for groups of that size.

What is the price for chartering the Azimut 58 in Marbella?

The Azimut 58 Flybridge is priced on request via WhatsApp — quotes within minutes for your date and group size. All prices include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% Spanish VAT.

Does the Sunseeker Predator 60 have a flybridge?

No, the Sunseeker Predator 60 does not have a flybridge. It features a large open cockpit with a sun pad aft and a helm deck with a retractable hardtop, offering a more integrated indoor-outdoor feel. The Azimut 58, by contrast, has a dedicated flybridge level.

Where do these boats depart from in Marbella?

Both the Azimut 58 Flybridge and the Sunseeker Predator 60 depart from Puerto Banús, the famous marina in Marbella. This puts you right at the heart of the Golden Mile, with easy access to the coast towards Estepona, Cabopino, and Sotogrande.

Which boat is better for a luxury day charter?

For a luxury day charter, the Azimut 58 Flybridge offers more deck space, a flybridge for sunbathing and dining, and a more relaxed cruising experience. The Sunseeker Predator 60 is better for those who prioritise sporty looks and a more intimate, cockpit-focused layout.

Can I book the Sunseeker Predator 60 through Boat Rental Marbella?

The Sunseeker Predator 60 is not currently listed in our fleet. We offer the Azimut 58 Flybridge as a premium option. For other similar-sized yachts, please contact us via WhatsApp to discuss availability and pricing.
